title: Phage Stock Preparation
author: Marijn Ceelen
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.7kthkwn
materials: Determine phage titer using plaque or spot assay.

steps.title: Grow E
steps.instruction: Grow E. coli bacterial host (for example LE392, DH10B or DH5alpha) in LB medium overnight at 37 °C

steps.title: Prepare and autoclave 1 Molarity (M)...
steps.instruction: Prepare and autoclave 1 Molarity (M) CaCl2 and 1 Molarity (M) MgCl2

steps.title: Add 50 µL of 1 Molarity...
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL of 1 Molarity (M) CaCl2 and 50 µL of 1 Molarity (M) MgCl2 to 50 mL of LB medium. Make aliquots of 10 mL and inoculate with 0.1 volumes of overnight bacterial host.

steps.title: Add 100 µL of high titer...
steps.instruction: Add 100 µL of high titer phage lysate (> 10 8 PFU/ml).

steps.title: Incubate at 37 °C with agitation...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37 °C with agitation for ∼ 05:00:00 or until lysate clears.

steps.title: Collect phage lysate and store at...
steps.instruction: Collect phage lysate and store at 4 °C until clean up.

steps.title: Pool phage lysate into 50 mL...
steps.instruction: Pool phage lysate into 50 mL sterile falcon centrifuge tubes and centrifuge at 4,000 rpm for 00:25:00 .

steps.title: Filter-sterilize the phage supernatant using a 0
steps.instruction: Filter-sterilize the phage supernatant using a 0.22 μm filter to yield a bacterial cell-free phage lysate.
